channel.title: The Car Jury, News
channel.description: News and verdicts on the Indian car market from The Car Jury.

item.title: Volkswagen Enters Sri Lanka With Made-in-India Taigun And Virtus
item.description: Skoda Auto Volkswagen India launches the VW brand in Sri Lanka with Chakan-built Taigun and Virtus, opening a 3S facility near Colombo.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: E25 Petrol As India's Default In 2-3 Years: Mileage Will Drop, Prices Won't
item.description: Government is in talks with automakers to make E25 petrol India's standard fuel in 2-3 years. Here is what it means for your car and your wallet.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mumbai CNG hits Rs 86/kg: second hike in May erodes the fuel's case
item.description: Mahanagar Gas raises Mumbai CNG by Rs 2/kg to Rs 86, the second hike this month. The running-cost gap to petrol keeps narrowing.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Jeep Borrows Tata's ARGOS Platform: India Becomes Jeep's Global Factory
item.description: Stellantis confirms an all-new Jeep SUV on the Tata Sierra's ARGOS platform, built in India for 50-plus markets, launching in 2028.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: SAIC Hands JSW The Keys: What An Indian-Controlled MG Means For Buyers
item.description: SAIC will sell another 10% in JSW MG Motor India to JSW, making the Jindal group the largest shareholder. Here is what it means for MG buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Jeep Teases Mystery SUV: Tata ARGOS-Based India Project Or NG Renegade?
item.description: Stellantis teased a new Jeep SUV at Investor Day 2026. Is it the next-gen Renegade or the Tata ARGOS-based India project? The Car Jury weighs in.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2026 Honda City Facelift: Sharper Face, Same Hybrid Smarts, Still The Thinking Buyer's Sedan
item.description: Honda has launched the 2026 City facelift with a new fascia, cabin tweaks and the e:HEV hybrid carried over. Here is our verdict for Indian buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: JSW Takes The Wheel At MG India As SAIC Cuts Stake To 39 Percent
item.description: SAIC is selling another 10 percent of JSW MG Motor India to JSW Group, making the Indian partner the largest shareholder. Here is what it means.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Toyota Ebella E3 At Rs 23.60 Lakh: Rs 3.60 Lakh Tax For A Badge
item.description: Toyota Ebella top E3 trim launched at Rs 23.60 lakh, Rs 3.60 lakh more than the identical Maruti e Vitara. The Car Jury's verdict on the badge premium.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Toyota Shelves Lexus LF-ZC EV: A Win For The Hybrid Bet, Not A Surrender
item.description: Toyota has reportedly halted the Lexus LF-ZC electric sedan as global EV demand cools, joining Honda and Nissan in trimming EV plans.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata's Design Boss Is Right: Budget Buyers Won't Settle Anymore
item.description: Tata Motors' Martin Uhlarik says even entry hatchback buyers now demand premium design. We agree, and the refreshed Tiago is the proof point.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2026 Tata Tiago Lands From Rs 4.69 Lakh: Safer Shell, Smarter Cabin, Still The Value Pick
item.description: 2026 Tata Tiago Petrol and iCNG launched from Rs 4.69 lakh and Rs 5.79 lakh ex-showroom, with a reinforced shell and new cabin. TCJ verdict inside.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Bets On New Tiago EV And CNG To Revive India's Dying Hatchback
item.description: Tata Motors launches new Tiago EV and CNG variants to revive India's shrinking hatchback segment. The Car Jury on whether the strategy holds up.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Toyota Ebella EV Launch Cancelled in India: A Self-Inflicted Wound
item.description: Toyota has reportedly cancelled the Ebella electric SUV launch in India and is refunding bookings with interest. The Car Jury's verdict.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata EV Bookings Jump 2.5X As Fuel Prices Bite: Sub-Rs 15 Lakh Is The Sweet Spot
item.description: Tata Motors says EV bookings have jumped 2 to 2.5 times in two months as West Asia tensions push fuel prices up. Capacity is going to 15,000 units.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: BYD Dolphin G DM-i Debuts For Europe With 1,000 km Range: India Next?
item.description: BYD unveils the Dolphin G DM-i, a 4.1m plug-in hybrid hatch with 1,000 km range for Europe. India hint follows. Our verdict on what it means.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: New Tata Tiago at Rs 4.69 Lakh: The BaaS Trick That Reframes the Hatchback War
item.description: Tata launches the new Tiago from Rs 4.69 lakh petrol and Rs 6.99 lakh EV, with a BaaS plan that drops the EV entry to Rs 4.69 lakh plus battery rent.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: MG Majestor vs Fortuner: Bigger Numbers Won't Crack Toyota's Grip
item.description: MG Majestor launches February 12, 2026 with a longer wheelbase and bigger cabin than the Toyota Fortuner. We weigh size against reliability.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti Serviced 2.84 Crore Cars in FY26: The Real Moat Isn't Sales
item.description: Maruti Suzuki serviced 2.84 crore vehicles in FY26 across 5,926 touchpoints. The Car Jury on why this network is the brand's real moat.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Lotus Emira 420 Sport: AMG four-pot, 3.9sec to 100, and the Emira finally bites
item.description: Lotus reveals the Emira 420 Sport with a 420hp AMG turbo-four, 3.9sec 0-100kph, lighter body and aero upgrades. Here is what it means for India buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: VinFast VF8 Patent in India: Paperwork, Not a Product Plan
item.description: VinFast has filed a design patent for the second-gen VF8 in India, but a launch is far from confirmed. Here is what the filing actually signals.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Honda City SV Costs 1.5-2 Lakh More Than Slavia, Virtus Base, And It Is Worth It
item.description: Honda City facelift base SV starts at Rs 11.99 lakh, well above the Slavia and Virtus base. Here is why the premium is defensible.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Skoda Kylaq Heading To Europe: India's Sub-4m Win Becomes A Global Card
item.description: Skoda is considering exporting the India-made Kylaq to Europe at under Euro 20,000 to counter Chinese rivals. Here is what it means for buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Honda Gives Thailand A Sporty City RS Hybrid While India Settles For Less
item.description: Honda launches the City facelift in Thailand with a sporty RS hybrid variant and a broader e:HEV line-up that India does not get.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: VinFast VF 8 Patented In India: Flagship SUV Incoming, But Does It Make Sense?
item.description: VinFast has patented the 2026 VF 8 in India, hinting at a flagship electric SUV above the VF 7. Here is what it means for buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: MG Majestor Lands At Rs 40.99 Lakh: Big On Paper, Wrong Badge For Fortuner Money
item.description: MG Majestor launches in India at Rs 40.99 lakh ex-showroom. The Car Jury on whether this Fortuner rival deserves your money.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Honda City SV Costs More Than Slavia, Virtus Base: But You Get What You Pay For
item.description: Honda City facelift's base SV is Rs 1.5-2 lakh dearer than Slavia and Virtus base trims, but packs an 8-inch screen and more kit. TCJ's take.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Sedan Sales April 2026: Dzire Eats Two-Thirds Of The Segment, City Quietly Grows 22%
item.description: Sedans grew 22% YoY in April 2026 to 35,218 units. Maruti Dzire took 67% share; Honda City posted 22% growth. The Car Jury verdict.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Skoda Kylaq Europe Export Plan: India's Sub-4m SUV Could Save VW From Chinese Onslaught
item.description: Skoda is reportedly evaluating the India-built Kylaq for European export under Euro 20,000 as VW Group fights Chinese rivals. TCJ's take.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Honda's Six-Car India Plan: Real Reset Or Another Round Of Promises?
item.description: Honda has outlined six new launches for India including the Zero Alpha EV and an Elevate facelift. The Car Jury's verdict on the new roadmap.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti Victoris CNG Discounts Hit Rs 2.10 Lakh: Read The Fine Print Before You Sign
item.description: Maruti Victoris CNG dealer benefits stretch up to Rs 2.10 lakh in May 2026, but most of that figure is conditional. Here is what to actually expect.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Renault Bridger Patent Filed In India: Chennai-Built Boxy SUV Targets 2027
item.description: Renault has filed patents for the Bridger SUV in India ahead of a 2027 launch. The boxy, Chennai-developed SUV will arrive in five versions.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: MG Majestor Enters Production: The Gloster's Replacement Means Business, But Can It Dent The Fortuner?
item.description: MG Majestor production begins with bookings open at Rs 41,000. A 212bhp diesel 4x4 ladder-frame SUV gunning for the Toyota Fortuner. Our verdict.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2026 Honda City V Variant At Rs 13.29 Lakh: The Sensible Sedan Buy
item.description: The 2026 Honda City facelift V variant is now Rs 13.29 lakh, up Rs 30,000. Here is why it remains the smartest pick in the sedan lineup.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Virtus GT Owner Books Himself: 200kph Instagram Reel Ends In Arrest
item.description: A Hyderabad Virtus GT owner filmed himself doing 200kph on the ORR, posted it to Instagram and got arrested. TCJ's take on the stupidity.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2026 Mercedes S-Class Facelift Lands June 15: PHEV-Only is the Right Call
item.description: Mercedes confirms June 15 India launch for the 2026 S-Class facelift, expected as a plug-in hybrid priced around Rs 2 to 2.4 crore.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Tiago CNG Facelift Cabin Revealed: 360 Camera, Wireless Charging, Paddle Shifters
item.description: Tata Tiago CNG facelift interior revealed before May 28 launch with 360 camera, dual wireless chargers, paddle shifters and rotary dial.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Honda Wants A Partner In India: The April Sales Sheet Tells You Why
item.description: Honda CEO Takashi Nakajima says the brand is open to India partnerships. April 2026 sales of 4,069 units explain the urgency behind that signal.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Renault-Nissan April 2026 Sales: Duster Revival Doubles Volumes, But Base Is Tiny
item.description: Renault and Nissan posted 8,616 combined units in April 2026, nearly double YoY, led by the new Duster and Nissan Magnite. The Car Jury's take.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Renault Bridger Patent Filing: The Jimny Finally Has a Real Rival Coming
item.description: Renault has patented the Bridger SUV in India. The Car Jury's take on why this sub-4m lifestyle SUV worries Maruti and Mahindra more than they admit.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Tiago Facelift Spied Undisguised: Sharper Face, But Wait For The Full Picture On May 28
item.description: The 2026 Tata Tiago and Tiago EV have been caught undisguised in Mumbai ahead of their May 28 launch. Here is what changes and our early take.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Hatchback Sales April 2026: Maruti's Stranglehold Is Now Embarrassing for Rivals
item.description: Hatchback sales in April 2026 grew 29.3% YoY to 95,907 units. Maruti took seven of the top eleven spots. The Car Jury's verdict on why rivals are losing.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Volkswagen's India Pipeline Is Thin and Late, and the Virtus Facelift Won't Fix That
item.description: Volkswagen has four cars lined up for India led by the Virtus facelift in Q3 2026. We say the plan is too cautious to move the needle.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: SBI's Rs 8,000 Crore Bet On JSW Motors Is The EV Industry's Real Stamp Of Arrival
item.description: SBI has committed Rs 8,000 crore to JSW Motors over a 10-year tenure. The Car Jury on what this means for MG buyers and India's EV race.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti's Up To Rs 30,000 June Hike: Book Now If You're Near The Showroom
item.description: Maruti Suzuki raises prices by up to Rs 30,000 across Arena and Nexa from June 2026. Our verdict: lock in your booking before month-end.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra Owns The 4.5-4.8m SUV Segment: Scorpio And XUV7XO Bury The Competition In April 2026
item.description: Mahindra Scorpio and XUV7XO took 63 percent of the 4.5-4.8m SUV segment in April 2026. Tata, Toyota and MG have a problem.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2026 Honda City Facelift Is Still The Thinking Sedan Buyer's Best Bet At Rs 12-21 Lakh
item.description: The 2026 Honda City facelift starts at Rs 12 lakh with the ZX+ e:HEV hybrid at Rs 21 lakh. TCJ's verdict on whether the variant spread makes sense.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mid-Size SUV Sales April 2026: Creta Holds, Victoris Storms In, Sierra Arrives
item.description: Mid-size SUV sales jumped 72% YoY in April 2026 as Creta led, Victoris debuted at No.2 and Sierra entered. The Car Jury's verdict on what to buy.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Next Creta With Pleos Connect: Hyundai Is About To Out-Tech Every Mid-Size SUV
item.description: The 3rd-gen Hyundai Creta spied with Pleos Connect single-screen cockpit signals a tech lead over Seltos, Taigun and Grand Vitara in India.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Honda ZR-V e:HEV Is Honda's Premium Reset, But CBU Pricing Will Decide Its Fate
item.description: Honda ZR-V e:HEV bookings open in India with 22.8 km/l claimed mileage, Level-2 ADAS and CBU import route. The Car Jury's verdict on what it means.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)
